behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real speech-to-text model labeling behavior (AI output quality), which a stubbed transcript would mask. Real HTTP/system effects exist, but the failure boundary here is model output fidelity, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets a single model outcome ('speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels'), not multi-agentic sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio file with known speaker turns; call the production STT API and assert returned transcript includes stable speaker labels aligned to known attendees at key utterances.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Would not reliably fail at Integration with mocked STT because label-quality failures are hidden.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic parsing/structuring logic on transcript text and does not require real OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment. Rejected Integration: behavior text is local transformation ('parsing extracts ... into ... structure'), not inherently a cross-module handoff failure.
behaviors.test_description: Given transcript snippets containing explicit assignments (e.g., 'Alex will send the deck by Friday'), assert parser outputs normalized records with assignee and task text fields in the summary-input schema.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A single-function/object test can catch malformed extraction without extra infrastructure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: The pass/fail outcome depends on what the real LLM includes in generated summary content for each assignee; deterministic fixtures can hide omission failures. Rejected Workflow: can be validated with a fixed transcript and one real summarization step (no required multi-agentic chain in behavior text).
behaviors.test_description: Feed a transcript fixture with explicit task assignments for multiple attendees into the live summarization path and assert generated structured summary contains at least one action item per assigned attendee.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration with mocked LLM would miss real omission/coverage failures.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets deterministic routing/mapping logic after decisions are already identified. Rejected Agentic using boundary token 'identified decisions': model judgment is out of scope here; this test checks schema placement logic.
behaviors.test_description: Provide intermediate input containing pre-identified decisions and narrative text; assert mapper places decision items only in `decisions` schema fields and not in generic narrative fields.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: No real infra or model output is required to reproduce this failure.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks whether the real LLM correctly identifies and places key decisions in generated output; stubs can force perfect placement and hide failures. Rejected Workflow: behavior can be tested from transcript-to-summary in a single real summarization decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Use real summarization on transcripts containing clear decision statements; assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as only narrative mentions.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Deterministic mocked model outputs would not expose true decision-identification/placement failures.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires multiple sequential processing steps with merge semantics across segments; in this pipeline those steps involve repeated model-driven transcription/summarization decisions. This is a multi-agentic sequence, so Workflow is the minimum. Rejected Agentic due to explicit sequence boundary tokens 'splits into segments', 'sequentially', and 'merging the results'.
behaviors.test_description: Run a >60-minute real meeting recording through production segmentation settings; verify segment-by-segment processing order, successful merge, and final structured summary coherence across cross-segment references.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single-call Agentic tests cannot catch sequencing/merge regressions across multiple segment runs.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end multi-stage pipeline with at least two model-dependent stages (transcribe then summarize) plus orchestration to delivery. Failures can emerge from cross-stage agentic handoff and sequencing, so Workflow is required. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text explicitly spans full chained pipeline from upload through delivery.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production-like pipeline on a real meeting audio file from upload to delivered structured payload; assert unattended completion, valid schema output, and non-empty key sections (action items, decisions, attendee attributions).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower levels cannot expose end-to-end orchestration and cross-stage model-interaction failures.
